title: PR-1 integrated GameSession exit gates
summary: Run and harden the integrated PR-1 gates for continuous 10-minute runtime, same-session read models, Worker/headless hash parity, 100000-tick leak checks and static-fixture retirement evidence.
acceptance: Normal speed GameSession runs continuously for 10 minutes with Worker-owned scheduling and without UI advance helpers, drain helpers or command-drain loops driving normal time
At least 8 default visible residents, focused resources, alerts and selection details are proven to come from one GameSession basis and matching world/read-model hash lineage
Worker and headless produce matching checkpoint hashes and read-model hashes for a fixed PR-1 seed and command stream
A 100000 tick integrated run reports no job, reservation, queue, path-request or resource conservation leak; first divergence diagnostics include seed, tick, owner store and reason

Default product route does not read WEB_PRODUCT_GATE_READ_MODEL or reviewed-playable-session as gameplay truth; remaining fixture paths are test/diagnostic-only and mechanically checked where feasible
Non-instant work produces observable moving and working projections across multiple publications; no resident work chain completes entirely within one visible frame
Idle, blocked and failed states in the PR-1 path carry structured reason codes and parameters rather than prose-only strings

Schema-v3 GameSession projection negotiation, exact version acceptance, malformed/newer fail-closed behavior and exclusion of legacy regression projections from the default product route are independently verified
The report lists every store reused in PR-1 and every historical M1-M8 capability still not connected to the product runtime
Existing M1-M8 deterministic, save/replay, Worker parity, package-boundary, platform-security and quality gates are either passing or any reviewed exception is explicitly recorded
No PR-2+ implementation, protocol/schema change beyond ADR-0017, save mutation, release action, telemetry/account surface or public save compatibility promise is added

history.details: Scoped as the PR-1 integrated gate task after Worker/Web routing; remains proposed and owned by qa-performance for independent evidence.
Made WM-0165 the sole direct dependency on the serial critical path and added independent schema-v3 projection negotiation/failure-closure evidence; task remains proposed and unclaimed.

PR-1 exit gates passed: focused 40/40, Worker smoke 18/18, Web shell 13/13, 18000/100000 ticks zero leaks, quality 458/458; report lists reused stores, unconnected M1-M8 capabilities and residual risks

changes_requested: High: the 10-minute Worker-owned scheduler gate is not proven. The real Chromium Worker smoke stops at tick >=9, while the 18000-tick command is headless explicit stepping; the report itself states no browser/Worker ran for 600 wall-clock seconds, so continuous scheduler survival/cadence/backpressure over the required interval remains unverified. High: the moving/working visibility gate fails in current behavior. The scheduler advances 3 ticks per publication; GameSessionJobLifecycle sets moving then working within tick 0 and completes by tick 3. An independent runtime probe observed tick 0 all idle and tick 3+ completed/idle, with no moving or working projection in any publication. Do not lower either gate; route a product/runtime repair plus focused Worker/Web evidence. Other required gates pass, including 100000-tick zero leaks, parity, coherent 8-resident/resource/alert/detail basis, fixture isolation, schema-v3 fail-closed, inventory, Web 13/13, Worker 18/18 and quality 458/458. Reviewer exact 'corepack pnpm exec vitest' failed twice to resolve vitest, but the installed direct Vitest entry passed the same 4 files 40/40.

Two High PR-1 hard gates require an independent product/runtime repair task before WM-0166 can resume: (1) prove a real Worker-owned scheduler survives and advances continuously for 600 wall-clock seconds at normal speed, with cadence/backpressure evidence and no UI advance/drain/command-drain clock; current Chromium smoke reaches only tick >=9 while 18000 ticks are headless explicit stepping; (2) repair the runtime/publication lifecycle so non-instant work exposes moving and working states across multiple Worker publications under the 3-tick scheduler quantum; current lifecycle transitions within tick 0 and completes by tick 3, so publications observe idle only. Create a focused simulation-engineer/sim-worker product repair task owning required sim-core/sim-worker production paths, followed by qa-performance focused real Worker and default Web evidence. Do not lower thresholds or change protocol/schema/save.

Created WM-0167 simulation-engineer runtime/lifecycle repair then WM-0168 independent qa-performance real Worker/default Web evidence. WM-0166 now depends on WM-0168 and must remain blocked until both repair tasks are independently verified, integrated and done.
Resume procedure satisfied: WM-0167 runtime/lifecycle repair and WM-0168 real Chromium Worker/default Web evidence are independently verified, integrated, and done.

saveLoadStance: No public save compatibility is introduced. Gate evidence may test focused internal snapshots only if WM-0163/0164 produced them explicitly and fail-closed.
forbiddenPaths: feature implementation beyond focused gates, tests, diagnostics and reports
lowering PR-1 exit gates to pass a partial implementation
packages/sim-protocol/** changes or any protocol/schema/save format mutation
PR-2+ autonomous-life, command/building expansion, product UX redesign or content-scale implementation
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services, public feedback or public save compatibility actions

review.summary: High: the 10-minute Worker-owned scheduler gate is not proven. The real Chromium Worker smoke stops at tick >=9, while the 18000-tick command is headless explicit stepping; the report itself states no browser/Worker ran for 600 wall-clock seconds, so continuous scheduler survival/cadence/backpressure over the required interval remains unverified. High: the moving/working visibility gate fails in current behavior. The scheduler advances 3 ticks per publication; GameSessionJobLifecycle sets moving then working within tick 0 and completes by tick 3. An independent runtime probe observed tick 0 all idle and tick 3+ completed/idle, with no moving or working projection in any publication. Do not lower either gate; route a product/runtime repair plus focused Worker/Web evidence. Other required gates pass, including 100000-tick zero leaks, parity, coherent 8-resident/resource/alert/detail basis, fixture isolation, schema-v3 fail-closed, inventory, Web 13/13, Worker 18/18 and quality 458/458. Reviewer exact 'corepack pnpm exec vitest' failed twice to resolve vitest, but the installed direct Vitest entry passed the same 4 files 40/40.

singleWriterRule: Strict width one: only WM-0167 may write product runtime now; WM-0168 remains proposed until WM-0167 done; WM-0166 remains blocked until WM-0168 done.
